instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `assert-bounded` that signals an error if a numeric value is not within an inclusive range [lo, hi]. Evaluates the value once.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 SCORE))
            (UNLESS (AND (>= #:G1 0) (<= #:G1 100))
              (ERROR "Value ~S not in [~S, ~S]" #:G1 0 100))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 SCORE))
            (UNLESS (AND (>= #:G112 0) (<= #:G112 100))
              (ERROR "Value ~S not in [~S, ~S]" #:G112 0 100))
            #:G112)

--- stderr ---
